top_categories.workout_context: 559 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Formosa 900's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 559 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
559 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Formosa 900's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Formosa 900's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 559 miles.
Formosa 900's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
At Formosa 900, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Formosa 900. Train it until it feels boring.
Tempo is Formosa 900's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 559 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 559 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 559 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Formosa 900. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 559 miles of mixed terrain in Circumnavigation of Taiwan.
